title: Neomammillaria celsiana
taxonomy: Neomammillaria celsiana is a species of flowering plant in the cactus family, Cactaceae. It is native to Mexico and is a part of the Neomammillaria genus. The classification of Neomammillaria celsiana places it as a species within the Mammillaria subgenus Pachyphloe. The species is further classified as a member of the tribe Pachycereeae and the subfamily Cactoideae. The classification of Neomammillaria celsiana reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary relationships within the cactus family.
uses: Neomammillaria celsiana, like some other species in the Mammillaria genus, has been used in traditional medicine in Mexico, where it is native, for its potential health benefits. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may aid in the treatment of minor wounds and skin irritations. However, more research is needed to fully understand the medicinal uses and potential applications of Neomammillaria celsiana. Traditional healers have used the plant's extracts to treat various health issues, including fever and respiratory problems. It's essential to note that the use of Neomammillaria celsiana for medicinal purposes should be under the guidance of a qualified healthcare professional.
